title: What Is The Most Infectious Bloodborne Pathogen
source_filename: What Is The Most Infectious Bloodborne Pathogen.docx

qas.q: Which one of the following bloodborne pathogens is the most infectious?
qas.a: Hep B

qas.q: In which type of testing is a patient's name recorded with the test result?
qas.a: Confidential

qas.q: What do unprotected sex, having multiple sex partners, and intravenous drug use have in common?
qas.a: They are high risk activities that put you at risk of infection

qas.q: Which of the following can result in a false-negative HIV test result
qas.a: Testing before seroconversion occurs

qas.q: HIV and Hepatitis are transmitted through what modes?
qas.a: Blood and sexual contact

qas.q: What type of personal protective equipment should you wear when helping a patient who has been shot in the thigh?
qas.a: gloves, mask, eye wear, and a gown

qas.q: Which of the following is not true about cleaning?
qas.a: Wipe spills immediately, but wait several hours to disinfect the surface

qas.q: How should rescue breaths be given to patients during CPR?
qas.a: use a CPR shield

qas.q: What should be done when a sharps container is full?
qas.a: get a new puncture-proof container to dispose of sharps

qas.q: How are contaminated gloves removed?
qas.a: Turn inside out while removing and do not touch skin with outside of glove.

qas.q: What type of gloves are used for most medical assistant tasks?
qas.a: Non-sterile

qas.q: When should hands be washed when you will be wearing personal protective equipment?
qas.a: Before donning and after removing equipment

qas.q: James has just finished a procedure with a patient. What should he do with the disposable gown he is wearing?
qas.a: Dispose of it in a bio-hazardous waste container

qas.q: What type of personal protective equipment should you use when helping a patient with a small cut that is not bleeding?
qas.a: Gloves

qas.q: Infectious materials include blood and bodily fluids, tissue, and cell specimens, mucous membranes, and what?
qas.a: Non-intact skin

qas.q: In what document does a facility describe the measures being taken to reduce exposure to bloodborne pathogens at the facility?
qas.a: Exposure control plan

qas.q: Which of the following must be included in an exposure control plan?
qas.a: Procedure to follow after an exposure incident

qas.q: Which health care employees are eligible for free Hepatitis B vaccination?
qas.a: Those at risk of exposure to blood or bodily fluids

qas.q: What is the purpose of a sharps injury log?
qas.a: To record details of all exposure incidents that involve a sharp

qas.q: Which one of the following would be considered an exposure incident?
qas.a: Sticking your hand with a suture needle while stitching a wound,
